steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two independent clauses: "Jean Alexandru Steriadi and Stefan Dimitrescu were among a group of Romanian artists based in Balchik in the early twentieth century" and "[this] group is referred to as the Balchik School." The blank must separate them correctly. "Fittingly" is a transitional adverb, not a coordinating conjunction, so it cannot join two independent clauses with only a comma.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'century fittingly, this': No punctuation before "fittingly" runs the two independent clauses together — a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'century, fittingly, this': A comma before a transitional adverb cannot join two independent clauses; this creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'century. Fittingly, this': A period correctly ends the first independent clause. "Fittingly," then begins the second sentence as a transitional adverb set off by a comma. ✓
  • Choice D — 'century, fittingly. This': The comma before "fittingly" attaches it to the first clause, and the period after leaves the second clause without a transition word — both structurally awkward and nonstandard. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
